data: Acid-fast bacilli (AFB) smear and culture and
For culture: 8 weeks for negative culture.

abscess aspirate, abscess swab, ascitic fluid, bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL), urine (bladder, ureteric, nephrostomy, suprapubic), bone, bronchial aspirate, curettage,drainage, endotracheal aspirate, fluid, gastric lavage, hydrocele fluid, joint fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid(PDF), pericardial fluid, peritoneal fluid (non-PDF), pleural fluid, pus aspirate, sputum, tissue/biopsy, tracheostomy aspirate

abscess aspirate, abscess swab, ascitic fluid, bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL), urine (bladder, ureteric, nephrostomy, suprapubic), bone, bronchial aspirate, curettage,drainage, endotracheal aspirate, fluid, gastric lavage, hydrocele fluid, joint fluid, peritoneal dialysis fluid(PDF), pericardial fluid, peritoneal fluid (non-PDF), pleural fluid, pus aspirate, sputum, tissue/biopsy, tracheostomy aspirate
1. AFB smear negative cases but strongly suspicious of TB.
2. AFB smear positive cases but strongly suspicious of non-TB mycobacterial infection, e.g. in HIV patients, or contamination.
2. Microbiologically confirmed TB within past one year (when nucleic acids of non-viable mycobacteria may persist).
PCR will not be routinely processed for cases without prior request for or results of AFB smear.
Each request is subject to review before processing.

Limitation: For TB NAT: Presence of inhibitors and cross contamination may affect the accuracy of the result which should be interpreted in the context of the clinical condition and other laboratory findings. Itshould be supplemented with AFB culture. TB cannot be ruled out solely based on negative resultsespecially in extra-pulmonary specimens owing to the low sensitivity of TB NAT in these sites. This NATcannot differentiate between members of M. tuberculosis complex including M. tuberculosis and M. bovis BCG.
Collect pus, purulent exudate, aspirate, or wound biopsy specimens in a sterile container without fixative. DO NOT SEND SWABS unless there is no other means. Dry swabs are unacceptable.
If the specimen must be stored, refrigerate it immediately after specimen collection and after office hours.
